title.en: A mistake that changed how you work
title.es: Un error que cambió cómo trabajas

question.en: Describe a mistake you made that changed how you approach your work. Be specific about what the mistake cost and what you do differently now.
question.es: Describe un error que cometiste que cambió cómo abordas tu trabajo. Sé específico sobre lo que costó el error y qué haces diferente ahora.

must_include: a specific, named mistake — not 'I made an error in judgment' but the actual thing that went wrong and what it broke or cost
the real cost of the mistake: customer impact, downtime duration, dollar amount, deadline slip, or trust lost — stated with honest specifics
the candidate's personal accountability — they were the decision-maker or the last line of defense, not just a bystander
a concrete behavioral change the candidate made post-mistake — a new process, a checklist, a design habit, a communication pattern
evidence that the change stuck: a situation where the new behavior prevented a similar mistake
must_avoid: picking a 'safe' mistake that was trivially small or easily reframed as a positive ('my mistake was caring too much about quality')
describing the cost in vague terms after being specific about everything else — the cost is the most important part
attributing the learning to a senior person who told them what to do differently — the lesson must be self-derived
